actionable_feedback: Enable workbook parsing for verifiers (install/openpyxl) or export a small CSV containing the key computed columns used in ranking (shrinkage_pct, empirical_variance, key_eta, normalized empirical_variance, normalized key_eta, composite_priority, shrinkage_margin, expert_review_flag) so cell-level verification and formula execution can be performed.

Resolve the documentation/formula inconsistency: explicitly state whether the composite priority uses shrinkage_pct or the computed shrinkage_margin (65 - shrinkage_pct). Update both the modeling_note.md and the workbook formulas to match the stated definition.

Provide explicit aggregated remediation-effect calculations showing per-option effect_pct, how option effects combine (additive or multiplicative), and projected post-remediation scores for each record; include a short tie-breaker rationale comparing single options and feasible combinations to justify why O1+O2 is the smallest sufficient action.

Record the provisional weights used for the composite priority (severity_weight, recency_weight, exposure_weight), flag them as provisional in the workbook, and include a recommended process or canonical weights to finalize them.
Re-run the sensitivity checks programmatically using the exported CSV or with openpyxl enabled and attach the recomputed sensitivity results and any updated expert-review flags to the deliverables.

Attach an explicit verification checklist and small reproducible artifacts (CSV of derived columns, minimal code/notebook or formulas listing cell references) so graders/verifiers can reproduce the main numeric claims without needing to execute the full workbook.

feedback_summary: Artifacts satisfy the deliverable contract and include methods, calculations, sensitivity notes, and recommendations. Key limitations: workbook cell-level verification was blocked (openpyxl unavailable), a minor documentation/formula inconsistency (a 'shrinkage margin' is defined but the composite priority formula references raw shrinkage_pct), and the deliverable does not include explicit aggregated effect-to-threshold calculations proving the recommended remediation (O1+O2) is the smallest sufficient combination. These shortcomings reduce reproducibility and the ability to programmatically verify the 'smallest sufficient' claim.
